task.name: validate-ssd

metadata.description: Validate a Scene Specification Document against VEO model constraints, aspect ratios, resolution options, reference integrity, and structural consistency.

output.description: Validation report with pass/fail per check, details on failures, and summary counts.

step.name: Load SSD
step.description: Load and parse the Scene Specification Document.

actions.action: Read episodes/{episode_id}/ssd/scene-spec.yaml
Parse YAML. If parsing fails, abort with YAML syntax error details.
Initialize validation counters: total_checks = 0, passed = 0, warnings = 0, failed = 0

step.name: VEO Model Constraints
step.description: Check every VEO scene against model-specific duration and aspect ratio constraints.

actions.action: CHECK [VEO-DUR-3.1]: VEO 3.1 Duration Validity For scenes using VEO 3.1 (veo-3.0-generate-001): - Duration MUST be exactly 4, 6, or 8 seconds - If duration is not in [4, 6, 8]: FAIL
CHECK [VEO-DUR-2.0]: VEO 2.0 Duration Validity For scenes using VEO 2.0 (veo-2.0-generate-001): - Duration MUST be exactly 5, 6, 7, or 8 seconds - If duration is not in [5, 6, 7, 8]: FAIL

CHECK [VEO-AR]: VEO Aspect Ratio Validity For ALL VEO scenes regardless of model version: - Aspect ratio MUST be either "16:9" or "9:16" - No other aspect ratios are supported by VEO - If aspect_ratio is not in ["16:9", "9:16"]: FAIL

step.name: Resolution Validation
step.description: Verify resolution profiles match Nano Banana and VEO valid options.

actions.action: CHECK [RES-NB]: Nano Banana Resolution Options For scenes using Nano Banana / Imagen for frame generation: - Verify resolution is a valid Nano Banana option from the model constraints - Valid resolutions depend on the specific model variant - If invalid: FAIL with message showing valid options
CHECK [RES-VEO]: VEO Resolution Options For VEO scenes: - Verify resolution is valid for the model + speed combination - If invalid: FAIL

step.name: Character References
step.description: Verify all character references point to existing character directories.

actions.action: CHECK [REF-CHAR]: Character Refs Exist in characters/ Directory For each scene that references characters (via character_refs, prompt tokens, etc.): - Extract all character names/IDs referenced - Verify characters/{character_id}/ directory exists - If directory not found: FAIL - If characters/ directory itself is missing: WARN and skip this check

step.name: Environment References
step.description: Verify all environment references resolve to existing definitions.

actions.action: CHECK [REF-ENV]: Environment Refs Exist For each scene with environment references: - Verify the referenced environment definition exists - If not found: FAIL - If characters/environments/ directory is missing: WARN and skip

step.name: Dependency Integrity
step.description: Check for orphan dependencies and ensure all ref: paths resolve.

actions.action: CHECK [DEP-ORPHAN]: No Orphan Dependencies Scan all scene fields for cross-references to other scenes, clips, or assets. For each dependency reference: - Verify the target exists (either as a scene_id, clip, or file) - If target is missing: WARN "Orphan dependency: {source} references {target}"

CHECK [DEP-REF]: All ref: Paths Resolve Scan all string values in the SSD for the "ref:" prefix pattern. For each ref: path found: - Strip the "ref:" prefix - Resolve relative to project root - If file/directory does not exist: FAIL "ref: path '{path}' does not resolve"

step.name: Structural Consistency
step.description: Check internal consistency of the SSD structure.

actions.action: CHECK [STR-DUR]: Total Duration Matches Sum of Scene Durations calculated_total = sum(scene.duration for all scenes) declared_total = ssd.total_duration_seconds If calculated_total != declared_total: FAIL
CHECK [STR-COUNT]: Scene Count Matches If ssd.scene_count != length(ssd.scenes[]): FAIL
CHECK [STR-UNIQUE]: Unique Scene IDs All scene_id values must be unique. If duplicates found: FAIL
CHECK [STR-FIELDS]: Required Scene Fields Present Every scene must have: scene_id, duration, tool, prompt (or equivalent) If any required field is missing: FAIL
CHECK [STR-ORDER]: Scene Order Continuity Scene order numbers should be sequential starting from 1 with no gaps If gaps found: WARN

step.name: Compile Report
step.description: Compile all results into the validation report.

actions.action: Count totals: total_checks, passed, warnings, failed overall_status: "PASS" if failed == 0, "FAIL" otherwise
Ensure episodes/{episode_id}/validation/ directory exists
Write ssd-validation-report.yaml

task.output_schema:
# ssd-validation-report.yaml
episode_id: "{episode_id}" ssd_path: "episodes/{episode_id}/ssd/scene-spec.yaml" validated_at: "{ISO-8601 timestamp}"
summary: overall_status: "{PASS|FAIL}" total_checks: {int} passed: {int} warnings: {int} failed: {int}
checks: - rule_id: "{rule_id}" name: "{rule name}" severity: "{error|warning}" status: "{pass|warn|fail}" details: "{description of issue if failed, null if passed}" affected_scenes: ["{scene_id}", ...]
recommendations: - "{actionable recommendation for each failure}"

error.action: WARN: "characters/ directory not found -- character reference validation skipped."
WARN: "characters/environments/ directory not found -- environment reference validation skipped."
